# agentic-toolkit definition schema

<!-- DO NOT EDIT — generated by tools/schemagen from internal/definitions struct definitions. Run `go generate ./...` to refresh. -->

Every asset in `definitions/` parses into one of seven category structs. The struct definitions in `internal/definitions/types.go` are the canonical schema; this document is regenerated from them.

## Layout overview

| Category | File path | Shape |
|----------|-----------|-------|
| skill | `definitions/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` | Markdown + YAML frontmatter |
| rule | `definitions/rules/<name>.md` | Markdown + YAML frontmatter |
| instruction | `definitions/instructions/<name>.md` | Markdown + YAML frontmatter |
| agent | `definitions/agents/<name>/AGENT.md` | Markdown + YAML frontmatter |
| command | `definitions/commands/<name>.md  (nesting allowed: definitions/commands/<group>/<name>.md)` | Markdown + YAML frontmatter |
| hook | `definitions/hooks/<name>.yaml` | YAML manifest (no body) |
| mcp | `definitions/mcp/<name>.yaml` | YAML manifest (no body) |

## Common fields

Every definition embeds the same `Common` base. Category is determined by the file's directory under `definitions/`; there is no `type:` field in YAML. `platforms` is an allowlist — omit it when the definition works on every platform; only set it to *narrow* (e.g. a Claude-only skill that depends on Claude features). When set, every populated `extensions.<platform>` block must reference a listed platform.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `name` | `string` | no | Optional. If present, must equal the path-derived name. If absent, derived from the file path. |
| `description` | `string` | **yes** | One-line summary used by adapters and discovery surfaces. |
| `platforms` | `[]Platform` | no | Target platform allowlist. Omit when the definition works on every platform; only set this to *narrow* (e.g. a Claude-only skill that depends on Claude features). |
| `tags` | `[]string` | no | Free-form tags for grouping and filtering. |
| `requires` | `[]string` | no | Cross-references to other definitions in 'category/name' form (e.g. skills/challenge). |

## Skills

**Path:** `definitions/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`  
**Shape:** Markdown + YAML frontmatter

A skill is a reusable, self-contained piece of agent capability — a prose body that an adapter renders into the platform's native skill format. Skills live in their own subdirectory so they can ship bundled resources (templates, allowlists, scripts) alongside SKILL.md.

### Frontmatter fields beyond Common

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `extensions` | `SkillExtensions` | no | Per-platform extension blocks. |

### `extensions.claude` (ClaudeSkillExt)

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `allowed_tools` | `[]string` | no | Tool allowlist for the skill (Claude-specific). |

### Example

```yaml
---
name: my-skill
description: Short description shown in tooling.
---

# Skill body

Markdown content rendered into the platform skill format.
```

## Rules

**Path:** `definitions/rules/<name>.md`  
**Shape:** Markdown + YAML frontmatter

A rule is a guidance document that platforms attach to the agent's context — either always-on or scoped to matching files. Canonical scoping uses doublestar globs in the `paths` field; adapters translate to per-platform syntax.

### Frontmatter fields beyond Common

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `paths` | `[]string` | no | Doublestar globs scoping when this rule auto-attaches. Empty + always=false means manual-only. |
| `always` | `bool` | no | If true, the rule always applies regardless of paths. |
| `extensions` | `RuleExtensions` | no | Per-platform extension blocks. |

### `extensions.cursor` (CursorRuleExt)

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `description` | `string` | no | Cursor-specific description override emitted into .mdc frontmatter. |

### Example

```yaml
---
description: Use 4-space indentation in TSX files.
paths:
  - "**/*.tsx"
---

When editing TSX, prefer 4-space indentation.
```

## Instructions

**Path:** `definitions/instructions/<name>.md`  
**Shape:** Markdown + YAML frontmatter

An instruction is prose concatenated into the platform's top-level instruction file (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, copilot-instructions.md) inside an `agtk:start`/`agtk:end` managed region. Instructions carry no scoping or extensions — they are global by design.

### Example

```yaml
---
description: Standing instruction concatenated into AGENTS.md.
---

Always commit with a Co-Authored-By trailer.
```

## Agents (subagents)

**Path:** `definitions/agents/<name>/AGENT.md`  
**Shape:** Markdown + YAML frontmatter

An agent is a named subagent the parent agent can delegate to. Canonical fields cover the common ground across Claude/Cursor/OpenCode; platform-specific knobs (memory, isolation, sampling, etc.) sit under `extensions`. Agents live in their own subdirectory so they can ship companion files (prompts, tools, fixtures) alongside AGENT.md.

### Frontmatter fields beyond Common

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `model` | `string` | no | Model shorthand: inherit, sonnet, opus, haiku, or a full model id. |
| `tools` | `[]string` | no | Tool allowlist (Claude tool-name vocabulary). Empty = inherit. |
| `color` | `AgentColor` | no | UI color hint. One of: red, blue, green, yellow, purple, orange, pink, cyan. |
| `extensions` | `AgentExtensions` | no | Per-platform extension blocks. |

### `extensions.claude` (ClaudeAgentExt)

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `disallowed_tools` | `[]string` | no | Denylist applied before the canonical Tools allowlist. |
| `permission_mode` | `string` | no | Claude permission mode override (default, acceptEdits, plan, ...). |
| `max_turns` | `int` | no | Hard cap on agentic turns. |
| `memory` | `string` | no | Persistent memory scope: user, project, or local. |
| `background` | `bool` | no | Run as a background task. |
| `effort` | `string` | no | Effort level: low, medium, high, xhigh, max. |
| `isolation` | `string` | no | Set to 'worktree' to isolate the agent in a git worktree. |
| `initial_prompt` | `string` | no | Auto-submitted first turn when run as the main agent. |

### `extensions.cursor` (CursorAgentExt)

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `readonly` | `bool` | no | Restrict the agent to read-only tools. |
| `is_background` | `bool` | no | Run as a non-blocking parallel agent. |

### `extensions.opencode` (OpenCodeAgentExt)

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `mode` | `string` | no | OpenCode agent mode: primary, subagent, or all. |
| `temperature` | `float64` | no | Sampling temperature. |
| `top_p` | `float64` | no | Top-p nucleus sampling. |
| `hidden` | `bool` | no | Hide the agent from the picker (subagents only). |
| `steps` | `int` | no | Maximum iterations. |

### Example

```yaml
---
description: Code-review subagent.
model: sonnet
tools: [Read, Grep, Glob]
color: blue
extensions:
  claude:
    permission_mode: plan
---

You are a careful code reviewer.
```

### Notes

**Tools** uses the Claude tool-name vocabulary (Read, Grep, Bash, Edit, Write, Agent, …). Adapters map to other platforms' tool names where possible and warn-and-skip on unmapped names.

## Commands (slash commands)

**Path:** `definitions/commands/<name>.md  (nesting allowed: definitions/commands/<group>/<name>.md)`  
**Shape:** Markdown + YAML frontmatter

A command is a reusable prompt template invoked via slash syntax. Nested directories produce namespaced names: `commands/git/commit.md` is referenced as `commands/git/commit` in cross-references and translated to `/git:commit` (Claude) or `/git/commit` (OpenCode).

### Frontmatter fields beyond Common

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `model` | `string` | no | Model shorthand or full model id. |
| `tools` | `[]string` | no | Tool allowlist. Empty = inherit. |
| `argument_hint` | `string` | no | Free-form hint shown in the command picker (e.g. "<branch-name>"). |
| `extensions` | `CommandExtensions` | no | Per-platform extension blocks. |

### `extensions.copilot` (CopilotCmdExt)

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `mode` | `string` | no | Copilot chat mode: ask, edit, or agent. |

### `extensions.opencode` (OpenCodeCmdExt)

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `agent` | `string` | no | OpenCode agent that runs this command. |
| `subtask` | `bool` | no | If true, force execution as a subagent. |

### Example

```yaml
---
description: Run the project linter.
argument_hint: "[paths...]"
tools: [Bash]
---

Run lint on $ARGUMENTS.
```

### Notes

**Body interpolation portability:**

- `$ARGUMENTS` — portable across Claude and OpenCode; Cursor appends free text after the command name.
- `$1`, `$2` (positional) — OpenCode-only.
- `` !`cmd` `` (shell) and `@filename` (file injection) — OpenCode-only.

The parser does not validate body content — these are notes for portable authoring.

**Cursor caveat:** Cursor commands carry no frontmatter at all. The Cursor adapter strips frontmatter and writes the body verbatim, dropping `description`, `tools`, `model`, and `argument_hint` with a doctor-time warning.

## Hooks

**Path:** `definitions/hooks/<name>.yaml`  
**Shape:** YAML manifest (no body)

A hook attaches a handler to a lifecycle event. Canonical handler types are `command` (shell) and `prompt` (LLM call) — the universal pair across declarative-hook platforms. Claude-specific handler kinds (http, mcp_tool, agent) live under `extensions.claude`.

### Frontmatter fields beyond Common

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `event` | `string` | **yes** | Lifecycle event the hook attaches to. See SCHEMA.md for portable vs platform-specific events. |
| `matcher` | `string` | no | Regex (or pipe-separated list) matching the event payload. Empty = match all. |
| `handler` | `HookHandler` | **yes** | What to run when the hook fires. |
| `fail_closed` | `bool` | no | If true, a non-zero exit blocks the action; default is fail-open. |
| `timeout` | `int` | no | Timeout in milliseconds; 0 = platform default. |
| `extensions` | `HookExtensions` | no | Per-platform extension blocks. |

### `extensions.claude.agent` (ClaudeAgentHandler)

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `prompt` | `string` | **yes** |  |
| `model` | `string` | no |  |

### `extensions.claude.http` (ClaudeHTTPHandler)

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `url` | `string` | **yes** |  |
| `headers` | `map[string]string` | no |  |
| `allowed_env_vars` | `[]string` | no |  |

### `extensions.claude.mcp_tool` (ClaudeMCPToolHandler)

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `server` | `string` | **yes** |  |
| `tool` | `string` | **yes** |  |
| `input` | `map[string]` | no |  |

### `extensions.claude` (ClaudeHookExt)

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `http` | `ClaudeHTTPHandler` | no | Claude-only http handler config. |
| `mcp_tool` | `ClaudeMCPToolHandler` | no | Claude-only mcp_tool handler config. |
| `agent` | `ClaudeAgentHandler` | no | Claude-only agent handler config. |
| `status_message` | `string` | no | UI message shown while the hook runs. |
| `once` | `bool` | no | Fire only once per session. |

### `extensions.cursor` (CursorHookExt)

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `loop_limit` | `int` | no | Cursor-specific loop guard for hooks that re-trigger themselves. |

### `handler` (HookHandler)

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `type` | `HandlerType` | **yes** | Handler kind: command or prompt. |
| `command` | `string` | no | Shell command (handler type=command). |
| `prompt` | `string` | no | Prompt template (handler type=prompt). |
| `model` | `string` | no | Model override for prompt-type handlers. |

### Example

```yaml
name: log-tools
description: Log every tool invocation.
event: PreToolUse
matcher: "Bash|Edit|Write"
handler:
  type: command
  command: "echo $TOOL >> /tmp/agtk-tools.log"
fail_closed: false
timeout: 2000
```

### Notes

**Event vocabulary.** The parser does not validate event names; adapters do at render time and skip-with-warn for events they don't support. Three rough sets:

- **Portable** (Claude ∩ Cursor, normalized to Claude's CamelCase): `SessionStart`, `SessionEnd`, `PreToolUse`, `PostToolUse`, `PostToolUseFailure`, `SubagentStart`, `SubagentStop`, `Stop`, `PreCompact`, `UserPromptSubmit`.
- **Claude-only** (examples): `PostToolBatch`, `Notification`, `TaskCreated`, `PostCompact`, `PermissionRequest`, `PermissionDenied`, `StopFailure`, `InstructionsLoaded`, `ConfigChange`, `FileChanged`, `WorktreeCreate`, `WorktreeRemove`.
- **Cursor-only** (examples): `beforeShellExecution`, `afterShellExecution`, `beforeReadFile`, `afterFileEdit`, `afterAgentResponse`, `afterAgentThought`.

**OpenCode** does not support declarative hooks; OpenCode adapter generates a TS plugin shim from each hook definition (future slice).

## MCP servers

**Path:** `definitions/mcp/<name>.yaml`  
**Shape:** YAML manifest (no body)

An MCP server definition declares one Model Context Protocol server. Canonical transports are `stdio`, `http`, and `sse`; transport-specific fields are mutually exclusive (parser-enforced). Adapters merge the selected MCP definitions into each platform's native config (`.mcp.json`, `.cursor/mcp.json`, `.vscode/mcp.json`, `opencode.json`).

### Frontmatter fields beyond Common

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `transport` | `Transport` | **yes** | One of stdio, http, sse. |
| `command` | `string` | no | Executable for stdio transport. Supports ${VAR} expansion. |
| `args` | `[]string` | no | Arguments for stdio transport. |
| `env` | `map[string]string` | no | Environment variables for stdio transport. |
| `url` | `string` | no | Endpoint URL for http/sse transport. |
| `headers` | `map[string]string` | no | Static request headers for http/sse transport. |
| `oauth` | `OAuthConfig` | no | OAuth configuration for http/sse transport. |
| `extensions` | `MCPExtensions` | no | Per-platform extension blocks. |

### `extensions.claude` (ClaudeMCPExt)

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `headers_helper` | `string` | no | Path or command emitting JSON headers at connection time (Claude-only). |
| `ws_url` | `string` | no | WebSocket URL for Claude's ws transport (out of canonical). |

### `extensions.opencode` (OpenCodeMCPExt)

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `enabled` | `bool` | no | OpenCode-specific enable toggle. |
| `timeout` | `int` | no | OpenCode-specific connect timeout in ms. |

### `oauth` (OAuthConfig)

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `client_id` | `string` | no | Pre-configured OAuth client id. |
| `callback_port` | `int` | no | Fixed local port for the OAuth callback. |
| `auth_server_metadata_url` | `string` | no | OAuth metadata discovery URL override. |
| `scopes` | `[]string` | no | Requested OAuth scopes (canonical: array; Claude adapter joins to a space-separated string). |

### Example

```yaml
name: filesystem
description: Filesystem MCP server.
transport: stdio
command: "${HOME}/bin/mcp-fs"
args: ["--root", "${WORKDIR:-/tmp}"]
env:
  LOG_LEVEL: info
```

### Notes

**Server scope** (local/project/user) is decided by the consumer at sync time via `.agentic-toolkit/config.yaml`, not by the definition. A single MCP definition can be installed at any scope.

**Variable expansion.** Field values may use `${VAR}` and `${VAR:-default}` (canonical, matches Claude). Adapters translate to per-platform syntax (Cursor `${env:VAR}`, Copilot `${input:VAR}`/`${env:VAR}`).

## Presets

**Path:** `definitions/presets/<name>.yaml`  
**Shape:** YAML manifest (no body)

A preset is a named bundle of definition references — toolkit-side metadata that consumers select by name in their config. Presets are not renderable themselves; the resolver expands each preset's `definitions` list against the available sources. Presets are not in the Category enum and do not embed `Common`.

### Frontmatter fields

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `name` | `string` | no | Optional. If present, must equal the filename stem. |
| `description` | `string` | **yes** | One-line summary used in tooling and discovery. |
| `definitions` | `[]string` | **yes** | Ordered list of definition refs. Local form: 'skills/foo' (or 'commands/git/commit' for nested). External form: 'skills::<repo-url>.git/<bundle-path>[@<ref>]' — the '.git/' substring is the explicit boundary between repository URL and in-repo bundle path (e.g. 'skills::github.com/owner/repo.git/skills/foo@main'). |

### Reference grammar

Each entry in `definitions` is one of:

- **Local**: `<plural-dir>/<name>` — e.g. `skills/challenge`, `commands/git/commit`.
- **External**: `<plural-dir>::<url>[@<ref>]` — e.g. `skills::github.com/anthropics/skills/skills/skill-creator@main`. The optional `<ref>` is anything git accepts (branch, tag, sha); the parser does not classify it — that is the resolver's job.

### Example

```yaml
description: Default toolkit bundle.
definitions:
  - skills/challenge
  - rules/bare-repos
  - skills::github.com/anthropics/skills/skills/skill-creator@main
```
